$description: YSS (Yogoda Satsanga Society of India) visual language. Values grounded in yssofindia.org analysis (March 2026) with elevated design intent. YSS is a sister organization to SRF, not a subsidiary. This design language expresses modern Indian spirituality — clean white foundations with warm devotional moments. Two registers coexist: the Modern Voice (white, terracotta, Raleway — functional, welcoming) and the Devotional Voice (cream, gold, Merienda — spiritual, warm). Metaphor: the ashram campus — sunlit courtyards for gathering, warm prayer halls for the teachings, terracotta earth underfoot, temple brass catching the light.

color.$description: YSS color palette. Grounded in yssofindia.org Elementor globals (March 2026). The primary accent is terracotta — the red earth of Ranchi. The spiritual accent is devotional gold — aged temple brass, not bright. The site is white-forward with warm moments, not uniformly earth-toned.

terracotta.$description: YSS Terracotta. Primary accent. The rust-red earth of Ranchi, the clay of Indian tradition. Used for H1 headings, section titles, CTAs, language selector backgrounds. WCAG AA large text on white (3.8:1).

org.yogananda.design.evaluationTrigger: If yssofindia.org primary color changes from this terracotta.

terracotta-hover.$description: Terracotta deepened for hover and active states. The clay fired darker.

terracotta-light.$description: Warm peach. Terracotta's ambient glow for highlighted content blocks and warm surface accents.

devotional-gold.$description: Muted temple brass. The devotional accent — aged gold, not bright. Used for Quote of the Day titles, decorative text, spiritual emphasis. Distinct from SRF's bright halo gold (#dcbd23). WCAG AA large text on white (4.2:1).

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Extracted from yssofindia.org Quote of the Day section heading and Elementor global --e-global-color-ef030a3: #A87D25.
org.yogananda.design.evaluationTrigger: If YSS spiritual content surfaces change accent color.

devotional-cream.$description: Warm devotional cream. The color of aged manuscript paper, temple lamplight on white walls. Background for spiritual content sections (Quote of the Day, featured teachings).

orange.$description: Bright orange. Secondary accent for events, CTAs, urgency. The marigold garland's vivid center.

saffron.$description: Saffron. The color of renunciation in Indian tradition. Spiritual accent for meditative and devotional surfaces. YSS sannyasis wear saffron robes.

text-primary.$description: Primary text. The site uses strong, clear black text.

text-body.$description: Body text gray. Neutral, readable, approachable. The primary body text color across yssofindia.org content paragraphs.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Confirmed from yssofindia.org body paragraph computed style: rgb(102, 102, 102).

text-secondary.$description: Secondary text. Dimgray for captions, metadata, attribution, card subtitles.

text-warm.$description: Warm brown text. For use on warm backgrounds (devotional cream, clay, sandstone) where neutral gray feels cold. The earth-ink color.

bg-white.$description: Primary background. The site uses true white — the sunlit ashram courtyard.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Confirmed from yssofindia.org page background: rgb(255, 255, 255).

bg-neutral.$description: Very light neutral gray. Alternating section backgrounds.

bg-warm.$description: Warm clay. The reading surface background for immersive spiritual content. The ashram wall.

surface.$description: Neutral surface for cards and elevated elements.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: From yssofindia.org computed section background: rgb(241, 241, 241).

border-warm.$description: Warm border for devotional and earth surfaces. The color of dried clay.

link.$description: Content link blue. Bootstrap-inherited but present across the site. Consider mapping to terracotta in future redesign.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Confirmed from yssofindia.org content links: rgb(51, 122, 183). Bootstrap default.
org.yogananda.design.evaluationTrigger: If YSS moves away from Bootstrap/WordPress, evaluate whether links should use terracotta.

navy-accent.$description: Deep navy. Defined in YSS Elementor globals as accent, used sparingly for dark UI elements.

terracotta-opacity.$description: Terracotta at calibrated attention levels. Parallels SRF's gold-opacity system. How terracotta serves different roles from call-to-action to ambient warmth.

interactive.$description: CTAs, active links, primary buttons. Full terracotta presence.

decorative.$description: Section dividers, ornamental marks. Present but quiet.

ambient.$description: Scroll indicators, progress bars. Peripheral warmth.

highlight.$description: Focus outlines, hover backgrounds. Guiding, not grabbing.

subliminal.$description: Hover backgrounds on content cards. Barely visible warmth.

texture.$description: Paper texture overlay. The ghost of ashram walls.

typography.$description: YSS type system. Four families: Raleway (UI — the modern voice, the site's workhorse), Merriweather (reading — shared lineage with SRF), Merienda (display/devotional — YSS's distinctive handcrafted warmth for spiritual headings), Noto Sans (Indic scripts — unified variable font with Devanagari subset for all 9 supported languages).

reading-en.$description: English reading font. Merriweather — shared with SRF for consistency across the lineage. The teachings are the same; the reading experience should honor that.

reading-hi.$description: Hindi reading font. Noto Serif Devanagari at 20px (11% larger than Latin 18px). 1.9 line height for shirorekha clearance. ADR-080.

display.$description: Display/devotional heading font. Merienda — a handcrafted cursive that gives YSS spiritual content a warm, human touch. Used for 'Quote of the Day' titles and devotional section headers on yssofindia.org. Where SRF uses Lora (formal serif), YSS uses Merienda (informal warmth).

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Confirmed from yssofindia.org: Merienda used at 30px/500 with 0.5px letter-spacing for Quote of the Day heading.

ui.$description: UI font. Raleway — the workhorse of yssofindia.org. Geometric sans-serif used for headings, body text, buttons, navigation. Weight range 400–600.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Confirmed from yssofindia.org Elementor globals: primary, secondary, text, and accent typography all use Raleway.

ui-semibold.$description: UI semibold. For buttons, labels, navigation items. Raleway 600.

ui-hi.$description: Hindi/Indic UI font. Noto Sans (unified variable font) — not the standalone Noto Sans Devanagari. The site loads Noto Sans with Devanagari subset (U+0900-097F) via unicode-range. Covers all 9 supported Indic scripts. Slightly larger than Latin to match optical weight.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Confirmed from yssofindia.org: loads google-fonts/css/notosans.css (Elementor local Google Fonts). The @font-face uses font-family: 'Noto Sans' with unicode-range subsetting for devanagari, latin, cyrillic, greek, and vietnamese.

heading.$description: Page heading (H1). Raleway 500 at 30px with 0.5px letter-spacing. Terracotta color. From yssofindia.org.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Confirmed from yssofindia.org H1 computed style: Raleway 30px / 500 / 0.5px / rgb(187, 79, 39).

section-label.$description: Section label (e.g. 'VIDEO & AUDIO TEACHINGS'). Uppercase Raleway 400 in terracotta. From yssofindia.org.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Confirmed from yssofindia.org section heading computed style.

display-inspirational.$description: Devotional display font. Asar — ancient Indian stone-inscription aesthetic. Chisel on stone, temple walls, Ashoka's edicts. The font that says 'these words endure.' The only font in the ecosystem that renders both Latin and Devanagari in a single devotional voice. When a teaching appears in English and Hindi on the same page, Asar makes them one inscription. Self-hosted under OFL.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Asar is culturally native to YSS's Indian context in a way Papyrus never could be. Its stone-carved aesthetic connects to the physical tradition — inscriptions on temple walls, carved gateways, the permanence of the guru's words in stone. Dual-script (Latin + Devanagari) means bilingual devotional content renders as unified visual voice.
org.yogananda.design.evaluationTrigger: If YSS develops a custom devotional typeface rooted in its visual tradition, re-evaluate.

hero-quote.$description: Hero banner quote. Asar overlaid on ashram photography — the guru's words inscribed on the landscape. Self-hosted, dual-script. Replaces the generic serif previously used on yssofindia.org.

theme.$description: YSS themes. Five expressions of the YSS visual language, from bright modern to deep devotional. Each theme has a metaphor grounded in the ashram experience. All themes share the same token structure as SRF (bg, bg-secondary, text, text-secondary, border, gold, surface) where 'gold' is the accent color.

ashram.$description: The sunlit courtyard. White and terracotta. The default — clean, modern, welcoming. This is how yssofindia.org presents itself today.

sandstone.$description: The prayer hall's lamplight. Warm cream with devotional gold. For spiritual content surfaces — where the teachings breathe. The Quote of the Day aesthetic expanded to a full theme.

earth.$description: The garden path. Warm clay and terracotta. The most immersive warm theme — for deep reading of the teachings. Shared name with SRF earth theme.

night.$description: The evening aarti. Deep warm brown — the darkness of a temple interior when oil lamps are lit. Not cool navy — warm darkness.

devotion.$description: The inner sanctum. Deep saffron-brown with golden light. For meditative, immersive spiritual experiences. The color of closing your eyes in a candlelit temple.

motifs.$description: YSS visual motifs. Based on yssofindia.org analysis (March 2026).

divider.$description: Para-ornament: a stylized scroll/flourish divider used between content sections on yssofindia.org. Not rangoli-geometric as previously hypothesized — a more universal spiritual ornament with warm tones.

emblem.$description: YSS emblem — circular spiritual emblem used as site logo and footer decorative element on yssofindia.org.

guru-legacy.$description: PY-legacy footer motif: Paramahansa Yogananda portrait with 'The Spiritual Legacy of Paramahansa Yogananda' in decorative script. Positioned as an architectural anchor between navigation and copyright. A distinctly YSS pattern — the guru's presence as a UI element, not just content.

languages.$description: YSS serves 9 Indian languages plus English. The design system must support all Indic scripts available on yssofindia.org.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Language list and order from yssofindia.org Quote of the Day tabs: English, Hindi, Gujarati, Tamil, Telugu, Bengali, Marathi, Malayalam, Kannada. Added Gujarati (gu) — confirmed present. Removed Odia (or) and Assamese (as) — not found on current site.

container-max-width.$description: Max content width. 1170px from yssofindia.org Elementor --container-max-width.

widget-spacing.$description: Default gap between content blocks. From yssofindia.org Elementor --widgets-spacing.
